name: Waiahole Poi Factory
tagline: One of the last real poi factories on Oahu — fresh poi, laulau, and squid luau in a Windward valley setting.
mustOrder: Fresh poi — hand-pounded, creamy, the real deal
Laulau — pork and fish wrapped in taro leaves, steamed until melt-tender
Squid luau — squid simmered in coconut milk and taro leaves
parking: Small gravel lot right off Kamehameha Hwy. Easy to spot.
talkStory: One of the last real poi factories on Oahu, operating in the Waiahole Valley for generations. They make fresh poi the traditional way — hand-pounded from Windward-grown taro. The laulau and squid luau are deeply authentic Hawaiian food, not tourist luau fare. The setting is stunning — lush valley with the Ko'olau mountains as backdrop. Open limited hours (usually weekends and some weekdays), so always call ahead. Cash only. This is a cultural treasure, not just a restaurant.
